name: Mr. Richard Sarfo Danso
bio: Mr. Richard Sarfo Danso is a seasoned international energy investor and petroleum sector specialist with over three decades of experience in offshore oil and gas assets. Born on June 14, 1965 in Houston, he developed early exposure to the energy industry within one of the world’s leading oil hubs. He pursued a Bachelor of Science in Petroleum Engineering at the University of Texas at Austin, followed by a Master’s degree in Energy Economics from Rice University. He later completed executive-level training in energy strategy at Stanford University, strengthening his expertise in global energy markets and investment structures.
companyHistory: Dubai Petroleum Establishment (commonly referred to as Dubai Petroleum) traces its origins back to the early development of Dubai’s offshore oil industry in the 1960s. The company’s roots began when the Emirate of Dubai granted its first oil exploration concession in 1963 and commenced oil production from its Fateh offshore field in 1969, marking the start of commercial oil exports from Dubai waters. Based on these historical milestones, as of 2026, Dubai Petroleum is about 63 years old since its establishment in the early 1960s and about 57 years since first commercial production began. The company formally evolved into the Dubai Petroleum Establishment under a royal decree in the mid‑2000s (taking direct control of operations by 2007), but its foundational legacy in Dubai’s oil sector dates to the 1960s.
about:
BIOGRAPHY

Mr. Richard Sarfo Danso

Mr. Richard Sarfo Danso is a seasoned international energy investor and petroleum sector specialist with over three decades of experience in offshore oil and gas assets. Born on June 14, 1965, in Houston, he developed early exposure to the energy industry within one of the world’s leading oil hubs.

He pursued a Bachelor of Science in Petroleum Engineering at University of Texas at Austin, followed by a Master’s degree in Energy Economics from Rice University. He later completed executive-level training in energy strategy at Stanford University, strengthening his expertise in global energy markets and investment structures.

Mr. Danso began his professional journey in the late 1980s, entering the oil and gas sector during a period of significant transformation in global energy markets. In 1989, he made a strategic private investment into offshore petroleum operations under Dubai Petroleum Company, marking the foundation of his long-term portfolio in the Middle East energy sector. This investment, initially valued at $8.5 million, evolved into a mature, income-generating asset with a current valuation of approximately $24.8 million as of 2026.

Throughout his career, Mr. Danso has maintained professional affiliations and investment exposure with leading global energy companies, including ExxonMobil, Chevron Corporation, BP, Saudi Aramco, and Royal Dutch Shell. His work has spanned upstream exploration, offshore production partnerships, and strategic advisory roles within international oil markets.

His professional engagements have taken him across major global energy regions, including the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia, United Kingdom, Norway, Nigeria, and the United States, providing him with a broad perspective on both developed and emerging energy markets.

Mr. Danso is recognized for his long-term investment philosophy, emphasizing capital preservation, steady income generation, and strategic positioning within high-value oil concessions. His portfolio reflects a disciplined approach to energy investing, with a focus on assets that deliver consistent returns over extended periods rather than short-term speculation.

With estimated lifetime earnings exceeding $40 million from energy investments alone, Mr. Danso represents a model of sustained success in the global petroleum sector. His career continues to be defined by calculated risk management, deep industry knowledge, and a commitment to long-term value creation.
